title: cleanup: remove duplicate frontend logo assets
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Removes unused legacy logo files from `pollinations.ai/src/assets`.
- Keeps the live logo source at `pollinations.ai/src/ui/assets/logo.svg`, which is what `Logo.tsx` and the icon generator use.

## Evidence
- `pollinations.ai/src/assets/logo.svg` had the same SHA as `pollinations.ai/src/ui/assets/logo.svg`.
- `pollinations.ai/src/assets/logo-text.svg` had no references.

## Validation
- `rg -n "src/assets/logo|assets/logo-text|logo-text|src/assets/logo\.svg" pollinations.ai/src pollinations.ai/scripts pollinations.ai/public pollinations.ai/index.html --glob '!**/node_modules/**'`
- `npm run build` in `pollinations.ai` with a temporary symlink to the existing frontend `node_modules`
- `git diff --check origin/main`

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the hive
gist.blurb: Swept away some duplicate logo files from the frontend codebase to keep things tidy.
gist.summary: Removed unused legacy logo files from the frontend assets directory to reduce repository clutter.
gist.impact: Developers will find a cleaner assets folder with a single source of truth for the pollinations logo.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot and the nomnom creature cleaning up duplicate pictures/logos in a cozy room.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana, sweeping up duplicate pixelated logo pictures with a small broom. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of old files. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
